title: salvia blancoana var. lagascana
intro: Salvia blancoana var. lagascana, also known as White Sage, is a perennial shrub native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the sage family (Lamiaceae). This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Salvia blancoana var. lagascana include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. Herbal preparations of White Sage can be found in teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used to soothe sore throats and calm the nervous system.
